title: Per-query dynamic orchestration is named as AMS's design forcing function
content: The Sovee inversion of statistical machine translation — index globally, compose dynamically per-query, beat pre-trained universal capability with just-in-time targeted work at 250ms — is the design lineage AMS belongs to. AMS exists to be the substrate for orchestration topologies that are built per-query from indexed primitives, executed in milliseconds, then dissolved. This forcing function is the throughline that connects D0001 (tokens not messages), D0006 (dream-house wire), D0009 (stream as primitive), D0010 (observability as subscriber), and the vodka discipline overall. The principle does not introduce new wire decisions; it makes the existing coherence explicit and gives canon a forcing function to evaluate future wire changes against. Encoded as ams://canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration (Tier 2, semi-stable).

title: AMS success criteria locked: utility-to-self plus maintainable foundation; external adoption is not a criterion
content: The success bar for AMS is "meets our needs as substrate for our verticals (Truthkit and others), enables products that have value, and remains maintainable." External adoption is not a success criterion. Adoption-class risks (no-vertical-takes-the-bet, slice-legibility-to-capital, dial-tone-with-no-killer-phone) collapse under this criterion because the operator's verticals are the verticals AMS exists for. Maintainability becomes THE binding constraint, with no community to catch failures and no investor pressure to keep shipping. Journaled as a D rather than promoted to a canon D-doc this session; D0011 candidate for a follow-on session if the operator wants it codified beyond this entry.

title: Federation lives as a participant pattern: anybody can forward streams
content: A forwarder is a polymorphic subscriber on broker A that also writes on broker B — reads X's stream on A, owns and re-emits it on B. D0001 (tokens as wire unit), D0003 (per-account ownership), and D0009 (ownership-excludes-subscription) compose to make this work without the broker growing any opinion about peer brokers. The wire stays vodka. Federation is something participants do, not something the protocol mandates. Higher-layer concerns (provenance, loop prevention, trust transitivity, discovery bootstrap, latency stacking) belong above the wire as participant conventions, not as wire features.

title: Vodka discipline applies to premortem analysis itself, not just to wire design
content: The premortem exploration twice slipped into participant-layer concerns dressed as wire-layer concerns (federation forwarding metadata, broker-product features, N8N/LangChain positioning). Each was a layer-discipline failure on the analysis side, even though the underlying wire decisions stayed clean. The same discipline that keeps the wire empty must be applied to the analysis that evaluates the wire — most premortem items belong at one specific layer, and conflating layers inflates AMS's surface area while feeling like care. The corrective is the explicit layer vocabulary used in this exploration (wire / broker-product / participant / Truthkit) and the practice of naming the layer before naming the concern.

title: The dream-house design and cut-for-reality separation extends to scoping decisions
content: D0006 is usually read as a runtime adaptation principle (wire stays clean, edge wrappers absorb runtime variance). It also governs scoping. Each "not now" decision in the cut-for-reality must be a deferral, not a delete. The cut should never include a choice that forecloses a dream-house future without anyone noticing. The premortem reframes accordingly: not "what fails if AMS becomes a public protocol" but "what choices in the cut quietly foreclose the public protocol becoming possible later." Vodka is the structural answer; silent erosion is the risk.

title: The existing wire decisions cohere around the per-query latency forcing function
content: D0001, D0006, D0009, and D0010 read as independent decisions on their own merits. Their coherence becomes visible once the per-query dynamic-orchestration forcing function is named: each preserves the per-query latency budget under a different pressure (writer concurrency, substrate portability, structural parallelism, cross-cutting concerns kept out of the hot path). The principle does not change any decision; it explains why the constellation looks the way it does, which is information that governs future "should we add X" calls more sharply than the individual decisions do alone.

title: The N8N/LangChain inversion framing is a special case of the Sovee inversion
content: N8N and LangChain assume the orchestration topology is known in advance and worth pre-building. The Sovee inversion says: build the topology per-query from indexed substrate, in milliseconds. AMS's emergent-topology-from-stream-subscription is the wire shape that enables this. The N8N/LangChain comparison was the right shape for the wrong reason during the exploration; the Sovee analogy is the actual reason. The "compatible but inverted force multiplier" framing the operator named lands as Truthkit-layer positioning that is sharper than the N8N/LangChain comparison was.

title: DO performance characteristics for AMS-Sovee shape are unmeasured
content: The latency-budget forcing function survives in the principle but is not yet validated on the reference impl. Specific items to benchmark against a Sovee-shaped synthetic workload: per-hop latency (target sub-15ms warm), conversation lifecycle cost (cold DO + magic-link issuance + two-door registration), stream creation and teardown cost, hibernation wake latency, cross-DO coordination cost when a per-query topology touches N conversations on N DOs, and geographic placement cost for non-colocated subscribers. Result determines whether DOs hit the budget for Truthkit-class workloads or whether the substrate-swap option (D0006) becomes the next move at the impl layer.

title: Control-plane reads can borrow the SHA-keyed three-tier cache pattern from the operator's other MCP work
content: The R2 + Cache API + memory cache pattern with SHA-keyed addressing — content-addressable, no-stale-data-by-construction, near-zero stacked latency on cache hit — is a read-path pattern. AMS's hot path is write-and-route, where DOs are the right primitive. But the AMS control plane (conversation metadata, membership, capabilities, magic-link validation) is read-heavy and currently lives in DO storage where it serializes on the DO's single thread. Moving control-plane reads to the SHA-keyed three-tier cache offloads work from the DO and frees it to focus on broadcast. Implementation-layer optimization that does not change the wire. KV is explicitly ruled out for any Sovee-shaped path due to consistency and cold-read latency.

title: Substrate-abstraction layer in the reference impl deserves a once-over while the codebase is small
content: The "trivial swap and replace" claim for substrate portability is operator-asserted and architecturally sound under D0006. Confirming the abstraction is actually wrapped cleanly in the reference impl now, while the codebase is small, is materially cheaper than confirming after Truthkit needs to scale. This is the option-preservation work that keeps the dream-house design viable if the chosen substrate cannot deliver the latency budget under load.

title: Sovee's global distribution via DYN DNS routing is the precedent for AMS global distribution
content: Sovee was global, not centralized — clients were routed to the nearest endpoint via DYN DNS-based geographic routing. The same playbook applies to AMS: per-region brokers (DOs pinned to colos), DNS-routed clients to nearest broker, federation-as-forwarding for cross-broker traffic. Global distribution becomes a known-pattern problem rather than an open architectural question. The cut-for-reality may use single-region brokers initially; the dream-house design preserves the option without prejudice.

title: The "compatible but inverted force multiplier to existing services" framing is sharper Truthkit positioning than the N8N alternative comparison was
content: Not competitive with N8N or LangChain — additive alongside them. "We don't replace your existing tools; we add agent-driven event orchestration that complements them via per-query dynamic composition." Easier sale, lower switching cost, no head-to-head with entrenched incumbents. Out of scope for AMS canon; flagged for Truthkit-side documentation when positioning work happens.

title: The Sovee inversion: index globally, compose dynamically per-query, instead of pre-training or pre-orchestrating universal capability
content: Sovee's statistical-machine-translation inversion stored the corpus in an Elasticsearch index and reverse-percolated each translation query against it to dynamically build the right corpus subset for that payload, then trained, tuned, and decoded in 250ms at higher quality than the global pre-trained models. The pattern generalizes: index everything, target the work per-query, beat pre-trained universal capability with just-in-time dynamic capability. Truthkit applies the inversion to agent orchestration: instead of declaring the workflow in advance, build the per-query agent topology from indexed participants, execute in milliseconds, dissolve. AMS exists to be the substrate that makes this cheap enough to be the default.

title: Layer vocabulary: wire, broker-product, participant, consumer-product (Truthkit)
content: Wire-layer is the AMS protocol — tokens, streams, magic links, broadcast rule, conformance. Broker-product-layer is the hosted broker as a product — pricing, buyer persona, dashboards, SSO, billing surfaces. Participant-layer is everything subscribers and forwarders do above the wire — provenance conventions, loop prevention, trust signing, discovery bootstrap, federation forwarding, recommended-conventions canon. Consumer-product-layer (Truthkit and other operator verticals) is what consumes AMS to deliver value to end users — the Sovee-inversion product, the inverted-force-multiplier positioning, the actual revenue/value path. Most premortem items belong at one specific layer; conflating layers is the most common analysis failure and inflates AMS's surface area while feeling like rigor.

title: Per-query dynamic orchestration principle drafted as Tier 2 semi-stable; operator commits
content: File: canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration.md. URI: ams://canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration. Tier 2, stability semi_stable. Does not introduce new wire decisions; explains the throughline of the existing ones (D0001, D0006, D0009, D0010, vodka) and gives canon a forcing function to evaluate future wire changes against. Operator commits the file. Verification after commit: oddkit_search with knowledge_base_url=https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service should surface the new URI, and overlay_doc_count should increment from 22 to 23. Long-form spec docs are not updated in this pass; that's a separate pass governed by SPEC §14.

title: Sovee-shaped benchmark workload queued against the reference impl
content: Action: build a synthetic workload that exercises the Sovee-inversion shape (conversation creation per query, multi-stream fan-out, short-lived streams, repeated query cycles) and measure per-hop latency, conversation lifecycle cost, stream lifecycle cost, hibernation wake, cross-DO coordination, and geographic placement cost. Result determines whether the reference impl hits the latency budget for Truthkit-class workloads, or whether the substrate-swap option (D0006) becomes the next impl-layer move. Owner: implementation phase, not this session.

title: Control-plane caching pattern queued as impl-layer optimization
content: Action: identify control-plane reads currently in DO storage (conversation metadata, membership, capabilities, magic-link validation), key them by SHA of dependency graph, route through R2 + Cache API + memory cache stack already proven elsewhere in the operator's MCP work. Wire decisions unchanged; impl gets the cache pattern that already works. Owner: implementation phase, not this session.

title: Success-criteria decision journaled as D, not promoted to canon D-doc this session
content: The "utility-to-self plus maintainable foundation; external adoption not a criterion" decision is load-bearing enough to journal as a D entry but is not promoted to a Tier 1 canon decision doc this session. D0011 candidate for a follow-on session if the operator wants it codified beyond the journal entry. Once codified, it would govern canon-scope-creep decisions more sharply than any of the existing constraints, because it makes explicit that the only constituency canon serves is the operator's own verticals.
